directions.horizontal_evidence: Frontal and centered, as expected for the vertical cardinal.
directions.vertical_evidence: Both amber eye surfaces and pupils press toward the upper lids; chin and face plane are visibly raised.

directions.horizontal_evidence: Face plane and near eye begin shifting screen-right, but blind consensus found the cue subtle.
directions.vertical_evidence: Eyes and chin remain clearly raised.

directions.horizontal_evidence: Nose, eye, and face plane project screen-right.
directions.vertical_evidence: Chin and eye line remain elevated.

directions.horizontal_evidence: Strong screen-right three-quarter face approaches the right cardinal.
directions.vertical_evidence: Raised eye/chin cue is present but one blind majority found this axis ambiguous.

directions.horizontal_evidence: Unmistakable screen-right profile with nose and chin beyond head center.
directions.vertical_evidence: Level pitch appropriate for the horizontal cardinal.
Eye line and chin begin a visible downward pitch.

directions.horizontal_evidence: Nose and visible face plane remain screen-right.
directions.vertical_evidence: Head and gaze tuck farther down.
Strong downward tuck lands before the down cardinal.

directions.horizontal_evidence: Frontal and centered, as expected for the vertical cardinal.
directions.vertical_evidence: Closed lowered lids and deeply tucked chin make down unmistakable.

directions.horizontal_evidence: Nose and face plane begin moving screen-left, but blind consensus found the cue subtle.
directions.vertical_evidence: Lowered eye line and chin clearly preserve down.

directions.horizontal_evidence: Nose, eye, and cheek plane project screen-left.
Stronger screen-left yaw approaches the left cardinal.
directions.vertical_evidence: Eye and chin remain below level.

directions.horizontal_evidence: Unmistakable screen-left profile with nose and chin beyond head center.
directions.vertical_evidence: Level pitch appropriate for the horizontal cardinal.

directions.horizontal_evidence: Face stays screen-left after the cardinal.
Nose and visible eye remain screen-left.
directions.vertical_evidence: Raised chin and eye line are clearly readable.

directions.horizontal_evidence: Subtle screen-left three-quarter face lands before frontal up.
directions.vertical_evidence: Eyes and chin remain raised for the wrap into 000.

ordered_loop_review: Pass: the independent final visual reviewer confirmed no wrong cardinal, wrong-quadrant pose, visible reversal, broken attachment, seam band, accidental filled-body hole, or conspicuous continuity pop. Intermediate blind ambiguity remains recorded as accepted warnings.
independent_review_note: All standard motions preserve Aemeath's pixel identity and state semantics; the 16-direction loop is coherent, unclipped, correctly anchored, and free of visible seams or accidental body holes.
